main-text: A platform for students and young professionals where solutions for the future of transportation are created: digitalization, infrastructure, logistics, and engineering innovation

1.caption: participants
1.text: Students and young professionals from different countries

2.caption: prize pool
2.text: Support for top solutions and expert evaluation of projects

3.caption: registration ends in
3.text: Join us and showcase your best solutions!

0.text: You will receive a real transportation company problem and go through the entire process from analyzing the situation to developing a full solution. It's like a startup, but in an intensive team immersion format.

1.text: You will work in a team, conduct interviews with real clients, investigate causes, generate ideas, create prototypes, consult with experts, and present your project.

2.text: This is real project-based work no textbooks or rote formulas, just real challenges, intelligence, creativity, and collaborative effort.

0.title: Registration
0.content: Register on the website to become a participant in the project competition.

1.title: Work
1.content: Over two weeks, participants analyze the situation, conduct interviews, develop a solution idea, and prepare a basic prototype.

2.title: Support
2.content: Communicate with experts and other teams in the case’s Telegram chat. Upload your stage results to your personal account.

4.title: Final in Moscow
4.content: Come to the three-day final for the best of the best.

problems-cards.card-situation-text: There is no effective metro cooling system, and the temperature rises every year due to increased passenger flow and the average air temperature.
problems-cards.card-problem-text: For testing, it is necessary to temporarily close a track branch, since the trolley is slower than regular and high-speed trains.

reasons-cards.0: Solve a real problem of a real company
reasons-cards.2: Build a portfolio you can show to employers
reasons-cards.stub-1: Opportunities that work for your future.

0.title: Who We Invite
0.text: We invite students from transport universities and other technical universities who are interested in solving real industry problems.

1.title: Who This Is For
1.text: If you want to test yourself in conditions close to real industry work, apply your engineering skills, and learn something new — this is definitely for you. It doesn’t matter what experience you already have — what matters is the desire to understand complex situations, offer unconventional solutions, and do it with reasoning.

faq-items.title: Who can participate?
faq-items.content: Students of any year enrolled in higher education programs at transport and technical universities and obtaining their first degree can participate. Teams with diverse skills — from analytics and engineering to design and IT — are encouraged.

faq-items.title: Do I need a ready project to participate?
faq-items.content: No. All teams start from the same point — a problem description. You will develop your solution step by step during the competition.

faq-items.title: What will participants do?
faq-items.content: You will investigate a real problem of a transport company, propose a solution, build a prototype, and present it to experts. The process follows a classic project flow: analysis → idea → validation → result.

faq-items.title: Can I participate individually?
faq-items.content: No, participation is only possible as part of a team. Each team represents one educational institution and may include students from different groups and years.

faq-items.title: How long does participation take?
faq-items.content: The full track lasts about 2 months: selection and registration take one month, followed by about a month to refine your solution, and a three-day final.

faq-items.title: What has changed this season?
faq-items.content: This year, the final format has been updated and a project expo has been introduced — teams present solutions in an engineering fair format. More real industry challenges have been added, along with technical support and new tools. The international track has been expanded with teams from other countries. Previous winners cannot participate again but may be invited to the international track.

faq-items.title: How many problems can a team solve?
faq-items.content: Each team can work on only one problem, and each participant can be part of only one team. The top 3 teams for each of the 7 challenges advance to the final.

faq-items.title: What do winners receive?
faq-items.content: The prize pool is 2 million rubles, along with gifts, diplomas, and most importantly — experience, connections, and new opportunities. Top teams may also be invited to future seasons and industry events.
